content: Comparing intrinsic curiosity methods like ICM and RND to general curious agents reveals that a curious agent’s open-ended exploratory drive can be framed as a dynamically-weighted ensemble of ICM’s prediction error-based novelty and RND’s state-based surprise, rather than a discrete alternative to either method. This re-framing lets researchers quantify how different general curious agents balance these two intrinsic reward mechanisms to adapt their exploration to long-horizon sparse-reward tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
